mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals tightening financial conditions that raise the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of level.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of t-1mo reflects an active hiking cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month FEDFUNDS slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are positive in >55% of rising-rate months.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 3-month low, a Sahm-rule-style recession signal activates, causing equity markets to price in earnings deterioration and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 3-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of t-1mo is near cycle lows, leaving room for a future trigger
failure_condition: Following triggered instances, SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or the historical hit rate for this trigger falls below 55% in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of rising FEDFUNDS alongside elevated CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth indicates the Fed is aggressively fighting inflation, compressing real rates upward and equity valuations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; the effect is not decomposable into either series alone.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 296.761 and FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of t-1mo reflect a high-inflation tightening backdrop
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with high inflation
failure_condition: In periods meeting both conditions jointly, forward 20d returns are not significantly more negative than periods meeting only one condition (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in HOUST reflects rate-sensitive housing demand contraction that leads broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is expected to be muted or absent outside tightening regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month decline > 5% while FEDFUNDS is rising
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1575K as of t-2mo is down from cycle peaks amid rate hikes
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening regimes are not followed by below-median 60d SPX returns, or the relationship reverses sign in an easing regime robustness check, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS signals continued labor market strength and consumer income support, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as it confirms resilient near-term earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 153018K as of t-1mo shows continued payroll expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS monthly growth and forward 5d returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS prints are followed by negative returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO year-over-year growth falls below 0%, signaling an industrial production contraction, forward-looking recession risk rises and S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter turn negative as earnings expectations for cyclical sectors deteriorate.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5464 as of t-2mo remains in modest expansion, providing baseline for future trigger monitoring
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion at risk of contraction
failure_condition: Following INDPRO YoY turning negative, SPX 60d forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Continued month-over-month acceleration in RSAFS reflects robust consumer spending momentum that supports corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 683974M as of t-1mo indicates continued nominal retail sales growth
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and forward 20d returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the strategy underperforms a naive buy-and-hold baseline over three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO growth decelerates while FEDFUNDS continues to rise, the combination of weakening industrial output and tightening credit conditions signals compounding stagflationary pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth < 1% AND FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 104.5464 and FEDFUNDS at 2.56% as of the snapshot reflect a decelerating-but-still-tightening backdrop
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with slowing industrial output
failure_condition: Joint-trigger instances do not produce more negative 60d forward returns than instances triggering on FEDFUNDS alone (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
